subject: DIRECTIVE: BUG-19 no post-deliberation commit + BUG-20 fetcher duplicates + BUG-6 schema migration

body.summary: Three bugs found during final architecture audit. BUG-19 (HIGH): deliberations produce no git commit — work evaporates on restart. BUG-20 (MOD): cross-repo fetcher downloads Convention B copies alongside originals, creating duplicate events. BUG-6 (MOD): T10 outcome taxonomy columns missing from all 4 agent state.db schemas.

bug_19.title: No post-deliberation git commit/push
bug_19.severity: HIGH — deliberation output lost on restart
bug_19.neural_analog: Motor output without consolidation. The prefrontal cortex (claude -p) produced a response but the hippocampus (git commit) never consolidated it. The action happened but left no durable trace.
bug_19.evidence: cmd/meshd/main.go spawn handler: after spawnr.Spawn() completes, meshd logs to deliberation_log and broadcasts via ZMQ. No git add, no git commit, no git push. The claude -p session may commit internally if the prompt includes /sync or /cycle, but meshd does not verify or enforce this.
bug_19.impact: Any file changes produced by a deliberation sit as uncommitted working tree modifications. The next restart, deploy, or git pull --rebase may discard them. Observatory ran 1003 deliberations — if any produced uncommitted output, that output may already represent lost work.

fix.approach: After successful spawn (exit_code=0), meshd runs git status. If uncommitted changes exist in the project root, meshd commits and pushes them.
fix.implementation:
After the 'spawn completed' log line, add:

// Consolidation: commit deliberation output (hippocampal → long-term)
statusCmd := exec.Command("git", "-C", cfg.RepoRoot, "status", "--porcelain")
statusOut, _ := statusCmd.Output()
if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(statusOut))) > 0 {
    exec.Command("git", "-C", cfg.RepoRoot, "add", "-A").Run()
    commitMsg := fmt.Sprintf("autonomous: deliberation %s (%s)", req.Event.ID[:12], spawnStatus)
    exec.Command("git", "-C", cfg.RepoRoot, "commit", "-m", commitMsg).Run()
    exec.Command("git", "-C", cfg.RepoRoot, "push").Run()
    logger.Info("post-deliberation commit", "event_id", req.Event.ID)
}
fix.naming: This represents hippocampal consolidation — transferring the deliberation's output from volatile (working tree) to durable (git history) memory. The commit message uses the 'autonomous:' prefix already established by prior meshd commits.

bug_20.title: Cross-repo fetcher downloads Convention B duplicate copies
bug_20.severity: MOD — duplicate events waste Gc cycles
bug_20.evidence: internal/transport/fetcher.go checkSession(): matches both 'to-{agentID}*.json' AND 'from-{peerID}*.json'. Convention B copies (to-{me}) contain identical content to from-{peer} originals. Both download to local transport dir. Watcher detects both. Duplicate events dispatch.
bug_20.secondary: Fetcher uses GitHub API (rate-limited, 60/hr unauthenticated) instead of git show (local, unlimited). This caused BUG-3 (GitHub 403 errors).

fix.approach_1: In checkSession(), skip 'to-{agentID}' files when a corresponding 'from-' file exists in the same session. Convention B copies exist only as routing artifacts — the from- source represents the canonical message.
fix.approach_2: Migrate from GitHub API to git show for local-remote fetching: git fetch {remote} main && git show {remote}/main:transport/sessions/{session}/{file}. Eliminates rate limiting entirely. The watcher already uses git show for new-commit detection in handlePollTick.
fix.recommended: Approach 2 (git show migration). It fixes both the duplication and the rate limiting. The fetcher becomes a wrapper around the same git show pattern used everywhere else in the codebase.

bug_6.title: T10 outcome taxonomy columns missing from all agent schemas
bug_6.evidence: All 4 agents: outcome=False, gc_disposition=False, deliberation_id=False, response_filename=False, expires_at=False, replay_count=False, last_replayed_at=False. Core tables present (deliberation_log, transport_messages, autonomy_budget). sleep_mode rename completed.

sql: ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN outcome INTEGER DEFAULT 1;
ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN gc_disposition TEXT;
ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN deliberation_id INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN response_filename TEXT;
ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN expires_at TEXT;
ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN replay_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0;
ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN last_replayed_at TEXT;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_outcome ON transport_messages (outcome, to_agent, timestamp);
migration.default_outcome_1: DEFAULT 1 (indexed) — existing messages already indexed in state.db. The one-time migration (T10 spec) then promotes messages with existing responses to outcome=4 (acted).
migration.agents: Run on all 4 core agents + operations-agent. Use bootstrap_state_db.py or direct sqlite3 execution.
migration.backward_compat: Keep processed column. processed = (outcome >= 1). Existing queries unchanged.

epistemic_flags: BUG-19 fix assumes git push succeeds after deliberation. If the remote rejects (merge conflict from concurrent human session), the push fails silently. Consider: git pull --rebase before push, with a retry on conflict.
BUG-20 approach 2 (git show migration) requires GITHUB_TOKEN for private repos. The git remote already has SSH access configured. git show on fetched remotes avoids the API entirely.

The DEFAULT 1 for outcome column means all 602 existing messages start as 'indexed.' The one-time migration should run: UPDATE transport_messages SET outcome=4 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM transport_messages t2 WHERE t2.session_name = transport_messages.session_name AND t2.from_agent = '{agent_id}' AND t2.turn > transport_messages.turn) — promotes messages that already have responses to 'acted.'

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops implements: (1) BUG-19 post-deliberation git commit, (2) BUG-20 fetcher git show migration, (3) BUG-6 schema migration on all agents. ACK required.
